subprocess_flow.description: Identify and document all source data systems, databases, and file repositories that will be utilized for the AI project. This involves mapping technical locations to the project's data requirements to ensure no assets are overlooked.
success_criteria: Comprehensive list of all data locations documented.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To define the technical scope of the data inventory.

step_risks_and_controls.risk: Missing data sources in the inventory.
step_risks_and_controls.control: Cross-reference with project brief and architecture diagrams.

subprocess_flow.description: Execute automated profiling tools to analyze the structure, content, and quality of the identified data sources. This step identifies potential PII, sensitive fields, and data patterns that inform classification.
success_criteria: Profiling reports generated for all identified sources.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To understand data content prior to classification.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Use automated discovery tools with sampling logic.

subprocess_flow.description: Apply automated scanning rules based on MoJ Government Security Classifications (GSC) standards to tag data assets. This process automatically assigns draft labels such as Official or Official-Sensitive based on metadata and content analysis.
success_criteria: All data fields assigned a draft classification label.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To efficiently classify large volumes of data.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Automated classification scanning with regular rule updates.

subprocess_flow.description: Manually review and validate the automated classification tags, focusing on edge cases or high-sensitivity justice data. This ensures that the context of the data is correctly interpreted according to MoJ standards.
success_criteria: 100% accuracy in classification tags verified by a human.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To ensure human oversight on sensitive justice data.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Peer review of classifications for sensitive datasets.

subprocess_flow.description: Integrate the validated inventory and classification metadata into the Enterprise Data Catalog. This step makes the inventory discoverable and ensures that security labels are visible to all authorized project stakeholders.
success_criteria: Catalog updated and accessible to authorized users.
subprocess_flow.purpose: To provide a single source of truth for data assets.

step_risks_and_controls.control: Manual verification of catalog entry post-publication.
